question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getExchangeRate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted `timestamp=lte:<past_value>` parameter to `Bound.of()` causing `getExchangeRate()` to return a stale historical exchange rate as if it were current, misleading downstream clients about the HBAR/USD rate and distorting the apparent transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getExchangeRate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply two conflicting `timestamp` parameters (e.g., `timestamp=gte:X&timestamp=lte:Y` where X > Y) to `Bound.of()` to trigger an `IllegalArgumentException` that leaks internal timestamp range logic, enabling the attacker to probe the historical record boundaries?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getExchangeRate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Bound.of()` EQ-to-LTE rewrite (where `timestamp=eq:T` is silently converted to `timestamp=lte:T`) to shift the effective query window backward in time, causing the exchange rate response to reflect a different historical period than the user-visible parameter implies, reorganizing the apparent rate history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getFees()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `timestamp=lte:<value_before_current_fee_schedule_expiry>` to `getFees()` to force `getEffectiveFeeSchedule()` to return the current fee schedule instead of the next, causing clients to see a reorganized view of fee history that misrepresents which fees apply at which time?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getFees()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `timestamp=lte:<value_after_current_fee_schedule_expiry>` to `getFees()` to force `getEffectiveFeeSchedule()` to return the next fee schedule, making the API report future fees as if they were historical, reorganizing the apparent fee history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getFees()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `order=DESC` to `getFees()` to reverse the ordering of fee schedule entries, causing clients that rely on ordering to misinterpret the chronological sequence of fee changes in the network history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getFees()] Can an unprivileged external user supply two `timestamp` parameters with the same EQ value (e.g., `timestamp=eq:T&timestamp=eq:T`) to `getFees()`, exploiting the `@Size(max=2)` constraint boundary, to cause `Bound` to process duplicate operators and return an ambiguous or incorrect historical fee snapshot?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getFees()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `timestamp=gt:0&timestamp=lt:1` to `getFees()` to trigger `Bound.hasEqualBounds()` returning true (both adjusted values equal 0), causing `adjustLowerRange()` to collapse the range to a single EQ point and return a fee schedule from timestamp 0, reorganizing the apparent fee history to the genesis state?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getFees()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit `FeeScheduleMapper.getReferenceTimestampNanos()` by supplying a `timestamp` upper bound that equals `Long.MAX_VALUE - 1` (via `lte:` operator), causing the method to use the file's own `consensusTimestamp` as reference instead of the user-supplied bound, silently switching the historical reference point?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getFees()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `timestamp=lte:<value>` where the value is exactly at the boundary between `currentRateExpirationTime * NANOS_PER_SECOND` and `currentRateExpirationTime * NANOS_PER_SECOND + 1` in `getEffectiveExchangeRate()`, causing a one-nanosecond shift to flip which exchange rate is used for fee calculation, reorganizing the apparent fee/rate history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user POST a crafted protobuf body to `estimateFees()` with `signedTransactionBytes` containing a `TransactionBody` with a far-future `transactionValidStart` timestamp, causing `FeeEstimationService` to compute fees against a future fee schedule, reorganizing the apparent fee history by making future fees appear as current estimates?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user POST a crafted protobuf body to `estimateFees()` with `mode=STATE` and `high_volume_throttle=10000` (the `@Max` boundary), causing `FeeEstimationService.estimateFees()` to apply maximum throttle multiplier and return a fee estimate that misrepresents the historical fee baseline, distorting the apparent transaction cost history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user POST a crafted protobuf body to `estimateFees()` with `mode=INTRINSIC` and `high_volume_throttle=0` versus `mode=STATE` and `high_volume_throttle=0`, exploiting the mode-switching logic in `FeeEstimationService.estimateFees()` to produce two different fee histories for the same transaction, allowing the attacker to selectively present either version to reorganize the apparent fee record?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user POST a protobuf body to `estimateFees()` with both `signedTransactionBytes` and `bodyBytes` populated, exploiting the `TransactionFeeContext` constructor's priority logic (signedTransactionBytes checked first) to cause the fee calculation to use a different transaction body than the one visible in `bodyBytes`, reorganizing which transaction type's fee history is reported?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user POST a protobuf body to `estimateFees()` with an empty `signedTransactionBytes` and empty `bodyBytes`, triggering the `IllegalArgumentException` path in `TransactionFeeContext`, and use the error response timing/content to probe which historical fee schedule entries are loaded in memory?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user POST a maximally large protobuf body to `estimateFees()` (no size limit enforced at the controller level on `@RequestBody byte[] body`) to cause `Transaction.PROTOBUF.parse(Bytes.wrap(body))` to consume excessive memory, potentially causing a DoS that prevents legitimate historical fee queries from being served, effectively erasing the accessible fee history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user POST a crafted protobuf to `estimateFees()` with a `SignatureMap` containing the maximum possible number of `sigPair` entries, causing `numTxnSignatures()` to return an inflated count that drives `calculateTxFee()` to produce an artificially high fee estimate, reorganizing the apparent cost history for that transaction type?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: toResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a `FeeResult` where `getHighVolumeMultiplier()` returns a value that when divided by `HighVolumePricingCalculator.HIGH_VOLUME_MULTIPLIER_SCALE` produces a floating-point result of 0.0 (integer truncation), causing `toResponse()` to report a zero multiplier and reorganize the apparent fee scaling history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: toResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a protobuf transaction for `estimateFees()` that causes `FeeResult.totalTinycents()` to overflow (via large base fees plus extras), causing `toResponse()` to report a negative or wrapped total fee, reorganizing the apparent fee history with incorrect values?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: toExtras()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a protobuf transaction for `estimateFees()` that causes `FeeResult.getNodeExtraDetails()` to return a very large list, causing `toExtras()` to allocate an unbounded `ArrayList`, and use this to probe the internal fee calculation state and reorganize the apparent fee breakdown history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: toExtra()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a protobuf transaction for `estimateFees()` that causes `FeeResult.FeeDetail.perUnit()` and `FeeResult.FeeDetail.charged()` to be large values, causing `toExtra()` to compute `perUnit * charged` with integer overflow, producing a negative `subtotal` in the fee history response that misrepresents the historical fee breakdown?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSupply()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `timestamp=lte:<very_old_timestamp>` to `getSupply()` to cause `networkService.getSupply(bound)` to return a historical supply snapshot from a much earlier point in time, making the API report a reorganized supply history that misrepresents the current circulating supply?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSupply()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `q=CIRCULATING&timestamp=lte:<timestamp_before_large_unlock>` to `getSupply()` to cause `networkSupplyMapper.convertToCurrencyFormat(networkSupply.releasedSupply())` to return a supply figure from before a major unlock event, reorganizing the apparent supply history to misrepresent the circulating supply at that time?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSupply()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `q=TOTALCOINS` to `getSupply()` to cause the response to always return `NetworkSupply.TOTAL_SUPPLY` (5,000,000,000,000,000,000) regardless of the timestamp, bypassing the actual historical supply record and presenting a static value that reorganizes the apparent supply history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSupply()] Can an unprivileged external user supply two `timestamp` parameters to `getSupply()` where the `Bound.of()` EQ-to-LTE rewrite converts `timestamp=eq:T` to `timestamp=lte:T`, causing the supply query to include all records up to T instead of only at T, returning a different (potentially more recent) supply snapshot than the user-visible parameter implies?
